app_subtitle: Plateforme d'évaluation de sécurité des appareils
tooltip_device_class: Architectural classification for risk assessment context (e.g. Broadcast Device vs. IT Device)
tooltip_manufacturer: Device manufacturer, e.g. Sony, Grass Valley, Riedel
tooltip_model: Model name and revision if applicable
tooltip_serial_number: Serial number from nameplate or SNMP OID
tooltip_ip_address: IPv4 or IPv6 address. Used for the Nmap scan.
tooltip_rdns_hostname: Automatically resolved hostname via reverse DNS
tooltip_mac_address: MAC address (auto-filled during scan if available)
tooltip_location: Physical location, e.g. Control Room 1, Rack A3
tooltip_network_segment: VLAN or network segment, e.g. BROADCAST-PROD
tooltip_owner_team: Team or person responsible for this device
tooltip_phpipam_id: ID in phpIPAM IP management system
scan_profile_passive_desc: Broadcast-specific ports only, T2, very gentle
scan_profile_standard_desc: All ports 1-65535, T3 – recommended for most devices
scan_profile_extended_desc: All TCP ports + key UDP ports (SNMP, RTP etc.)
scan_authorization_hint: Before starting an Nmap scan, operational authorization must be documented (per NIST SP 800-115 / IEC 62443-2-1).
scan_authorization_confirm: I confirm that operational authorization for this scan has been obtained and the scan risks are understood.
scan_warning_broadcast: Broadcast devices may be sensitive to network scans. The "Passive" profile is recommended for production devices.
encryption_warning: The same shared secret must be configured on the import system.
tooltip_default_creds_exist: Does the manufacturer ship the device with a predefined default password (e.g. admin/admin)? Check the vendor documentation or the device's first-login screen.
tooltip_default_creds_changeable: Does the device allow changing the factory-set password? Some broadcast devices have backdoor accounts that cannot be changed.
tooltip_individual_accounts: Does the device support multiple user accounts with individual credentials? Shared accounts (one login for all) make activity attribution impossible.
tooltip_roles_available: Can users be assigned different permission levels (e.g. Read-Only vs. Admin)? Required to implement the principle of least privilege.
tooltip_mfa_possible: Does the device support multi-factor authentication (e.g. TOTP, hardware token)? Especially important for management interfaces.
tooltip_central_auth_possible: Can the device integrate with a central authentication infrastructure (LDAP, Active Directory, RADIUS)? Enables centralised user management and quick account lockout.
tooltip_firmware_updatable: Can the device firmware be updated by the operator themselves? On some devices this is only possible via the manufacturer or a service partner.
tooltip_security_updates_available: Does the manufacturer provide security-only patches without requiring a full firmware upgrade? Reduces risk and effort for critical vulnerabilities.
tooltip_security_advisories: Does the manufacturer publish structured security advisories when vulnerabilities are discovered? Check the vendor website for a security or PSIRT section.
tooltip_lifecycle_documented: Has the manufacturer published a clear End-of-Life (no more updates) or End-of-Support (no more vendor support) date for this product?
tooltip_update_without_downtime: Can a firmware update be applied without interrupting broadcast operations for an extended period? E.g. via redundant systems, rolling update, or short reboot time.
tooltip_unnecessary_services_disableable: Does the device allow disabling services that are not needed (e.g. Telnet, FTP, SNMP)? Fewer open ports means a smaller attack surface.
tooltip_web_interface_disableable: If the management interface is accessible via HTTP/HTTPS: can it be disabled when not needed, or restricted to HTTPS only?
tooltip_insecure_protocols_disableable: Can protocols such as Telnet, FTP, SNMPv1/v2 and unencrypted HTTP be disabled? Ask the vendor if not evident from documentation.
tooltip_certificates_replaceable: Does the device allow replacing the factory self-signed certificate with a custom certificate (e.g. from an internal PKI)?
tooltip_tls_configurable: Can you configure which TLS versions (minimum TLS 1.2) and cipher suites are allowed? TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are considered insecure.
tooltip_syslog_supported: Can the device send security events (logins, errors, configuration changes) to a central syslog server? Essential for detection and incident response.
tooltip_snmpv3_supported: Does the device support SNMPv3 with authentication and encryption? SNMPv1/v2 transmit community strings in plaintext.
tooltip_login_logging: Does the device log successful and failed login attempts? A basic requirement for detecting brute-force attacks.
tooltip_config_change_logging: Is there an audit log recording who made which configuration change and when? Important for traceability and forensics.
tooltip_production_critical: Does a failure of this device directly cause a broadcast outage or significant quality degradation in live operations? This significantly influences the risk rating.
tooltip_redundancy_available: Is there an identical spare device or redundant solution that can take over automatically or manually in case of failure?
tooltip_fallback_possible: Can an alternative solution be switched to in case of failure (e.g. analogue backup, alternative signal path)?
tooltip_management_vlan_possible: Can the device's management interface be placed in a separate, production-network-isolated management VLAN? This is the most important compensating control.
tooltip_legacy_services_required: Are insecure protocols (Telnet, FTP, SNMPv1) unavoidably required for operational reasons? If yes: describe compensating controls in the next field.
tooltip_psirt_available: Does the manufacturer have a Product Security Incident Response Team (PSIRT) or at least a documented contact for security reports? Without this channel, coordinated vulnerability remediation is barely possible.
tooltip_hardening_guide_available: Does the manufacturer provide documentation explaining how to securely configure the device? Check the vendor website and ask support.
tooltip_security_roadmap: Has the manufacturer communicated how security topics will be addressed in future versions? Indicates the long-term security maturity of the vendor.
